claims.text: The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation is the most common mutation in this Malaysian IOPD cohort, occurring in 8 patients (10 alleles) with an allele frequency of 33%.

citations.quote: The most common mutation identified was c.1935C>A p.(D645E), occurring in eight patients (10 alleles) with allele frequency of (33%).

claims.text: Two unrelated patients were homozygous for c.1935C>A p.(D645E) (patients 13 and 17), and six patients carried it as compound heterozygotes.

citations.quote: Two unrelated patients were homozygous for the c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation. The other 13 were compound heterozygotes.

claims.text: Patient 13 (Chinese, homozygous c.1935C>A/c.1935C>A) presented at age 4 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; GAA activity 2.0%; LVEF 28%; LVMI 483 g/m²; with heart failure.

Patient 17 (Malay, homozygous c.1935C>A/c.1935C>A) presented at age 2 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; GAA activity 1.7%; LVEF 29%; with heart failure.

Patient 5 (Chinese) was compound heterozygous for c.2024_2026delACA p.(N675del) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E), presented at age 4 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; heart failure present.
Patient 6 (Chinese) was compound heterozygous for c.1843G>A p.(G615R) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E), presented at age 6 months with all major features; heart failure present.

Patient 7 (Chinese) was compound heterozygous for c.1411_1414delGAGA p.(E471Pfs*5) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E), presented at age 3 months; GAA activity <0.1 umol/h/L; LVEF 9.9%; LVMI 485 g/m²; heart failure present.

Patient 9 (Chinese) was compound heterozygous for c.1411_1414delGAGA p.(E471Pfs*5) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E), presented at age 3 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; heart failure present.
Patient 12 (Chinese) was compound heterozygous for c.2024_2026delACA p.(N675del) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E), presented at age 2 months; GAA activity 5.3%; LVEF 81.6%; LVMI 159 g/m²; no heart failure.
Patient 15 (Chinese) was compound heterozygous for c.2662G>T p.(E888*) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E), presented at age 4 months; GAA activity 0.6%; LVEF 33%; LVMI 461 g/m²; heart failure present.
The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation has been reported to cause both IOPD and LOPD, and has the highest allele frequency in Southern China (25%), Taiwan (36%), and Thailand (32.4%).

citations.quote: This mutation has been reported to cause both IOPD and LOPD [28, 29]. The Chinese patients in Malaysia share other common mutations with patients from China and Taiwan

claims.text: The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation is identified as the most common mutation in Southern China, Taiwan and Thailand, and the study cites it as supporting evidence of a founder effect.

citations.quote: This has also been reported to be the most common mutation in Southern China (25%), Taiwan (36%) and Thailand (32.4%) [25–27].

claims.text: Four patients in the cohort share the same c.1935C>A p.(D645E)-containing genotypes as patients from a Taiwan newborn screening study, but unlike those patients (who achieved independent walking), all four Malaysian patients died between ages 6 months and 3 years without achieving any motor milestone due to late treatment initiation.

citations.quote: Four of our patients share similar molecular mutations [two patients were homozygous for c.1935C>A p.(D645E), two patients were compound heterozygous for c.1411_1414del p.(E471Pfs*5) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E)] with patients in a study in Taiwan who were diagnosed via newborn screening and given very early ERT prior to symptom onset

claims.text: Patient 13 (homozygous c.1935C>A p.(D645E), classic IOPD) started ERT at age 8 months (4-month time lapse), achieved LVMI normalization: No, no motor milestones, required BiPAP ventilation and nasogastric feeding, died at age 2 years.

Patient 17 (homozygous c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) started ERT at 2.5 months (0.5-month time lapse) with immunomodulation; IgG antibody titer negative; LVMI normalization: No; no motor milestones; required CPAP; died at age 6 months.
The cohort had a median age at presentation of 3 months and median age of diagnosis of 6 months, and all 17 patients presented with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (100%).

citations.quote: Our cohort had a median age of presentation of 3 months and median age of diagnosis of 6 months. Presenting features were hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (100%), respiratory insufficiency (94%), hypotonia (88%), failure to thrive (82%), feeding difficulties (76%), and hepatomegaly (76%).
